commit.message:
fix(sidebar): use counts and minimal-list APIs DEV-1772 (#6918)

### 📣 Summary

Use new API for Sidebar lists of projects (Deployed, Draft, and
Archived). This fixes a problem when user with more than 200 projects
was unable to see all of their projects in the Sidebar (previously it
was limited to 200 latest projects, prioritizing Deployed ones). Now
three groups use infinite scroll loading. We also fixed a problem with
sidebar serving always "My Projects" projects - now when user switches
to organization projects or a custom view, the sidebar reflects this
properly.

### 💭 Notes

Changes here:
- `AssetName` and `getAssetDisplayName` now works with
`AssetMinimalList`
- `BulkDeletePrompt` invalidates all sidebar related queries now
- `SidebarFormsList` has multiple changes:
- It now operates on context (`my-projects`, `my-org-projects`, or
`custom-view-projects`) which is resolved from current path
  - Has a utility function for invalidating all queries in sidebar
- Has a custom `useQuery` (instead of using orval hooks) that operates
on different function based on context
  - Reflux actions are now invalidating with utility function
- It now only fetches counts from new APIs (different counts API for
different context) and passes those to `SidebarFormsListCategory`
- `SidebarFormsListCategory` has multiple changes too:
  - It operates now on context and deployment status
- Instead of getting list of assets from parent, it fetches it now using
API
- Has a custom `useInfiniteQuery` (instead of using orval hooks) that
operates on different function based on context and is triggered by
`InfiniteScrollTrigger`
- The list of projects now always lead to `ROUTES.FORM`, as minimal list
endpoint doesn't serve permissions

### 👀 Preview steps

1. ℹ️ Have an account with a lot of projects, ideally over 200 (use
kobo-fixture, or drag and drop a lot of small projects [many
small.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26484188/many.small.zip)
or some other idea)
2. [Enable
MMO](https://www.notion.so/kobotoolbox/Setting-up-MMOs-for-local-development-1357e515f6548093b631f1686b60e49d)
3. Create new user, add it to MMO, create few projects with the new
user, go back to original user afterwards
4. [Enable a custom
view](https://www.notion.so/kobotoolbox/Regional-Projects-User-Documentation-Setting-up-Custom-Project-Views-f45b8a2bd09d45a4ade89f4b4430df1d)
with only part of projects being included (see "South Asia" example in
the link)
5. Change few projects to have the "Country" field matching the custom
view
6. Go to My Projects
7. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab,
`api/v2/assets/?q=asset_type:survey&limit=200` is no longer being called
8. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab `/api/v2/assets/counts/` is being called
9. Open "Deployed" category
10. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab `/api/v2/assets/minimal-list/` is being
called and a list of projects appear
11. Scroll down
12. 🟢 Notice that another API call is being made and next 20 projects
are loaded (infinite scroll)
13. Select one project in the table (with checkbox)
14. Choose "Delete" button in top right and confirm modal
15. 🟢 Notice that both the count ("Deployed" + "(n)") and the list of
projects refreshed and deleted project no longer appears
16. Select two projects in the table (also with checkbox; bulk deleting
uses different code)
17. Choose "Delete" button in top right and confirm modal
18. 🟢 Notice that both the count ("Deployed" + "(n)") and the list of
projects refreshed and deleted project no longer appears
19. Select "My projects" dropdown and choose "<username>'s organization
projects"
20. 🟢 Notice sidebar refreshed and counts are slightly different (now it
includes projects from step (3))
21. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab,
`api/v2/organizations/<uid>/assets/counts/` is being called
22. Open "Deployed" category
23. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab
`api/v2/organizations/<uid>/assets/minimal-list/` is being called and a
list of projects appear
24. Proceed with "Delete" button steps 13-18 described above
25. Select "My projects" dropdown and choose "South Asia" custom view
26. 🟢 Notice sidebar refreshed and counts are slightly different
27. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab,
`api/v2/project-views/<uid>/assets/counts/` is being called
28. Open "Deployed" category
29. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab
`api/v2/project-views/<uid>/assets/minimal-list/` is being called and a
list of projects appear
30. Proceed with "Delete" button steps 13-18 described above

commit.message:
fix(api): Rate Limiter — Stale Bucket Eviction and Nested Lock Contention (#4592)

* Initial plan

* fix(api): rate limiter stale bucket eviction and nested lock contention

- Add TTL-based eviction in RateLimiter::allow(): buckets idle > 2×window
  with tokens >= capacity are removed to bound memory growth
- Replace std::mutex with std::shared_mutex in OperationRateLimiter so
  allow()/remaining()/getHeaders() take shared locks, setLimit()/clear()
  take exclusive locks — eliminates nested lock bottleneck under concurrency
- Compute steady_clock::now() before acquiring mutex_ in RateLimiter::allow()
  and remaining(); pass pre-computed time_point into Bucket::refill() and
  Bucket::consume() to keep clock syscalls outside the critical section
- Add <shared_mutex> include
- Update Bucket::refill() and Bucket::consume() signatures to accept
  a time_point parameter
- Add tests: StaleBucketEviction and ConcurrentReadsDontBlock

* fix(api): correct kMaxMessageSizeMbLimit to 2047 MB to prevent int overflow

2048 * 1024 * 1024 = 2,147,483,648 overflows signed 32-bit int (INT_MAX).
2047 * 1024 * 1024 = 2,146,435,072 fits safely within INT_MAX.

feat(uploads): enforce 10 GB per-user upload quota (#210)

* feat(uploads): enforce 10 GB per-user upload quota

Add per-user storage quota enforcement to the upload route. Before
storing a file to R2, compute the user's total upload usage by summing
attachmentSize from messages and directMessages tables. Reject uploads
that would exceed the 10 GB cap with a 413 response.

- Add getUserTotalUploadSize query with parallel Promise.all execution
- Add MAX_TOTAL_UPLOAD_SIZE constant (10 GB) and quota check in route
- Add user_email indexes on messages and direct_messages for query perf
- Add migration 0005_add_user_email_indexes
- Add unit tests for query and quota enforcement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(uploads): move quota check after authorization

Move the per-user quota check to run after channel/conversation
validation and access checks so that invalid or unauthorized requests
return the correct 400/403/404 status codes instead of being masked
by a 413 quota error.

commit.message:
S3: delete directories in batches (#12910)

We were iterating over each file when deleting a directory. boto3 has
support for batch operations, it does one call for every 1K files, this

feat: Add file command autocompletion and job stage detection

Two improvements:

1. Enable autocompletion for file operation commands (rm, cp, mv, du)
   - Added these commands to the file-browsing completion list
   - Users can now type 'rm ' or 'cp ' and Tab to browse files
   - Improves usability for file operations

2. Detect and display job stage in 'run check' command
   - New detect_job_stage() function identifies which job was submitted
   - Analyzes SLURM output file for script names and keywords
   - Falls back to checking script modification times
   - Displays stage (PRE, MAIN, POST, etc.) in the status output
   - Users can now see at a glance which stage was last submitted

Examples:
  • rm file.txt<TAB>
  • cp /path/to/file .<TAB>
  • run check Case001
    → Shows: Stage: MAIN, Status: SUCCESS

The improvements make file operations and job monitoring more intuitive.

before the max-iterations-exhausted check. When MARKDOWNLINT_MAX_ITERATIONS=1
and the loop breaks on the last iteration due to unparseable output, the
exhaustion branch no longer fires first. Also tighten the exhaustion guard
to require final_violation_count > 0.

commit.message:
E3: Pod restart detection via get_status health check

- Add Role::Unknown variant (#[repr(u8)] + TryFrom<u8>) to distinguish
  freshly started pods from explicitly demoted ones (Role::None)
- Add ReplicaStatusInfo struct and get_status() to ReplicaHandle trait
- GrpcReplicaHandle: calls existing GetStatus RPC, updates cached progress
- InProcessReplicaHandle: Mutex<Role> for type-safe role tracking
- Proto: ROLE_UNKNOWN=0 (default), ROLE_NONE=4

Reconciler Healthy phase now probes ALL replicas via get_status():
- Epoch mismatch or Role::Unknown -> stale (pod restarted)
- gRPC error -> stale (pod dead)
- Primary stale -> FailingOver (with stale secondaries cleaned first)
- Secondary stale -> remove handle, requeue for scale-up re-add
- Runs before switchover check (prevents switchover to dead target)

KvClusterApi mock: crash_pod() preserves data_dir (PVC simulation),
restart_pod() reuses it (PVC re-attach). Tests upgraded to crash_pod.

commit.message:
Runner error reclassification, pipeline gap telemetry, and tests (#68)

* reclassify runner-origin errors as TestStatus.Error and report via telemetry

- Add IsRunnerBug flag to TestResult to distinguish runner limitations
  (MockCodeunitHandle/MockRecordHandle InvalidOperationException) from
  genuine test failures (AlDialog.Error, MockAssert)
- Add IsRunnerError() helper in Executor: InvalidOperationException with
  AlRunner.Runtime.Mock frames → TestStatus.Error + IsRunnerBug=true
- Update PrintResults: runner bugs print a GitHub issue link hint instead
  of 'Inject via AL interface' (which is only for NotSupportedException)
- Add TryReportTestErrorsAsync to TelemetryReporter: after each run,
  groups runner-bug errors, shows one prompt, sends them to App Insights
- Refactor GetVersionString/GetOsString to shared helpers
- Wire TryReportTestErrorsAsync in Program.cs after pipeline.Run()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* unify pipeline gap telemetry: Roslyn exclusions + runtime errors in one prompt

Previously only runtime runner-bug errors were reported. Now ALL pipeline gaps
feed into one telemetry batch:

- Roslyn compilation exclusions (rewriter misses): when the BC-generated C#
  cannot compile after rewriting, the excluded file's Roslyn errors are sent
  as AlRunner.CompilationGap events. This surfaces rewriter gaps so they can
  be fixed proactively.
- Runtime gaps (AlRunner.RuntimeGap): InvalidOperationException from
  AlRunner.Runtime.Mock* — same as before but now deduplicated by message
  before sending (one event per unique error, not one per test).
- Single combined prompt shown once after each run covering both categories.
  No prompt if there are no gaps.

Also adds PrintCompilationGaps() to Executor: prints a WARN block to stdout
after test results listing excluded files with their Roslyn errors and a link
to file an issue. This replaces the quiet INFO log that previously went to
stderr, making rewriter gaps visible to the user.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* add tests for runner-error classification and isRunnerBug JSON field

Tests:
- MockCodeunitHandle_UnknownId_ThrowsClassifiableException: verifies the
  detection component — MockCodeunitHandle(99999).Invoke throws
  InvalidOperationException with 'not found in assembly' and
  'AlRunner.Runtime.Mock' in the stack trace, which is exactly what
  Executor.IsRunnerError() checks.
- PipelineResult_AssertFail_IsRunnerBugFalse: regression guard — user
  assertion failures (test-06) must NOT be classified as runner bugs.
- PipelineResult_TestPass_IsRunnerBugFalse: passing tests must have
  IsRunnerBug = false.
- JsonOutput_IsRunnerBugTrue_AppearsInJson: isRunnerBug:true appears in
  JSON for runner-limitation errors; absent (null) for pass/fail so the
  JSON stays clean for tooling consumers.
- PrintResults_IsRunnerBugTrue_OutputContainsHint: the 'Runner limitation'
  hint with the GitHub issues link appears in stdout for runner-bug errors.

Note: full end-to-end IsRunnerBug=true via the pipeline requires a
package-based scenario (codeunit available as symbol but not compiled into
the assembly). The detection component is covered by the direct Mock test.

Also adds isRunnerBug to SerializeJsonOutput — previously the field was
not included in --output-json output, making it invisible to tooling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix two review issues from PR #68

1. PrintCompilationGaps: route all output to stderr instead of stdout
   to avoid corrupting JSON when --output-json is active.

2. TelemetryReporter: apply ScrubMessage to each Roslyn error string
   in gapMessages before sending to App Insights. Roslyn diagnostics
   can include absolute file paths; this ensures the privacy claim
   ('no file paths sent') holds for compilation gap telemetry too.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Favicon badge: only show when page was unfocused, clear after 3s

- Replace unreadCount with badgeCount — only incremented when new
  notifications arrive while document.hasFocus() is false
- Add window focus/blur listeners: after 3 continuous seconds of focus
  the badge count is zeroed and favicon/title are cleared
- Blur cancels the pending 3s timer so focus time resets cleanly
- Dismiss no longer decrements the badge (it clears on focus instead)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix VLAN socket binding to secondary IP on multi-IP interfaces (#99)

In prepare_vlan_sockets(), the code iterates getifaddrs() and overwrites
client_addr with each IPv4 address found on the VLAN interface, ending
up with the last one. On interfaces with a secondary IP (e.g. Vlan1000
with 192.168.0.1/21 primary and 192.169.0.1/22 secondary), the socket
gets bound to the secondary IP, causing all client-facing DHCP responses
to use the wrong source address.

Fix: use addr_is_primary() (introduced in the parent commit) to skip
secondary IPs and break on the first primary IP found.

Signed-off-by: Xichen Lin <lukelin0907@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: add compact return summaries to all pipeline agents (#9)

Each agent now returns a structured JSON summary (schema v1) as its
final message instead of prose. Includes schema_version and warnings[]
for forward compatibility. Reduces orchestrator context accumulation
by ~50K tokens per pipeline run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: use canonical checklist reference in audit agents (#12)

Eliminates redundant cli-audit skill invocations and repeated file
reads. Auditors now read quality-checklist.md directly once at start
instead of invoking the skill. Single source of truth maintained.
Return summaries also added for both auditors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: add parallel resource dispatch to CLI generator (#11)

For CLIs with >5 resource groups, the generator now spawns per-resource
subagents in batches of 4. Shared modules (client, auth, types, errors)
are still built sequentially first. Caps per-subagent context at ~30K
tokens vs 120K+ for the monolithic approach.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: add immutable per-phase output artifacts (#13)

Orchestrator writes compact, immutable output manifests after each
phase (.cli-pipeline/phase-outputs/phase-NN-*.json). Later phases
read these ~200-500 token summaries instead of re-parsing large
artifacts. Schema-versioned for forward compatibility.
